verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual N-terminal lipid modifications: co-translational myristoylation at Gly-2 and post-translational palmitoylation at Cys-3. All functional domains (SH4, SH3, SH2, kinase) face the cytosol. Mutation of both acylation sites delocalizes Lyn to the cytoplasm, confirming that membrane association is entirely inner-leaflet. No TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no extracellular domain exist. Pre-no checklist: (1) no documented outer-leaflet/ecto-pool in cancer or oncogenic transformation; (2) caspase cleavage during apoptosis removes the lipid anchor and releases a cytosolic fragment — not an ecto-pool; (3) no germline/developmental outer-leaflet display; (4) activation-induced signaling occurs at the cytoplasmic face, not ecto-face; (5) not a lysosomal TM protein; (6) minor nuclear and Golgi pools are cytoplasm-facing; (7) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to extracellular face. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic (antibody/ADC/CAR-T) targets the LYN protein body from outside the cell.
